title: Ensure that the API Server only makes use of Strong Cryptographic Ciphers

input_row.title: Ensure that the API Server only makes use of Strong Cryptographic Ciphers
input_row.description: Ensure that the API server is configured to only use strong cryptographic ciphers.
input_row.rationale: TLS ciphers have had a number of known vulnerabilities and weaknesses, which can reduce the protection provided by them. By default Kubernetes supports a number of TLS ciphersuites including some that have security concerns, weakening the protection provided. Impact: API server clients that cannot support modern cryptographic ciphers will not be able to make connections to the API server.
input_row.audit: Run the following command on the Control Plane node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Verify that the --tls-cipher-suites argument is set as outlined in the remediation procedure below.
input_row.remediation: Edit the API server pod specification file /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml on the Control Plane node and set the below parameter. --tls-cipher- suites=TLS_AES_128_GCM_SHA256,TLS_AES_256_GCM_SHA384,TLS_CHACHA20_POLY1305_SH A256,TLS_ECDHE_ECDSA_WITH_AES_128_CBC_SHA,TLS_ECDHE_ECDSA_WITH_AES_128_GCM_SH A256,TLS_ECDHE_ECDSA_WITH_AES_256_CBC_SHA,TLS_ECDHE_ECDSA_WITH_AES_256_GCM_SH A384,TLS_ECDHE_ECDSA_WITH_CHACHA20_POLY1305,TLS_ECDHE_ECDSA_WITH_CHACHA20_POL Y1305_SHA256,TLS_ECDHE_RSA_WITH_3DES_EDE_CBC_SHA,TLS_ECDHE_RSA_WITH_AES_128_C BC_SHA,TLS_ECDHE_RSA_WITH_AES_128_GCM_SHA256,TLS_ECDHE_RSA_WITH_AES_256_CBC_S HA,TLS_ECDHE_RSA_WITH_AES_256_GCM_SHA384,TLS_ECDHE_RSA_WITH_CHACHA20_POLY1305 ,TLS_ECDHE_RSA_WITH_CHACHA20_POLY1305_SHA256,TLS_RSA_WITH_3DES_EDE_CBC_SHA,TL S_RSA_WITH_AES_128_CBC_SHA,TLS_RSA_WITH_AES_128_GCM_SHA256,TLS_RSA_WITH_AES_2 56_CBC_SHA, TLS_RSA_WITH_AES_256_GCM_SHA384. Default Value: By default the Kubernetes API server supports a wide range of TLS ciphers References: 1. https://github.com/ssllabs/research/wiki/SSL-and-TLS-Deployment-Best- Practices#23-use-secure-cipher-suites Additional Information: The list chosen above should be fine for modern clients. It's essentially the list from the Mozilla "Modern cipher" option with the ciphersuites supporting CBC mode removed, as CBC has traditionally had a lot of issues
input_row.impact: API server clients that cannot support modern cryptographic ciphers will not be able to make connections to the API server.
input_row.references: 1. https://github.com/ssllabs/research/wiki/SSL-and-TLS-Deployment-Best- Practices#23-use-secure-cipher-suites Additional Information: The list chosen above should be fine for modern clients. It's essentially the list from the Mozilla "Modern cipher" option with the ciphersuites supporting CBC mode removed, as CBC has traditionally had a lot of issues

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_api_server_tls_cipher_suites_restricted

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Query the kube-apiserver Pod specification on all control plane nodes. This is typically found in the static Pod manifest at `/etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml` and is represented as a Pod resource in the local kubelet.
- Use the Kubernetes API (via `kubectl get pod -n kube-system -o yaml` or direct file inspection if running outside the cluster) to extract the command/args for the kube-apiserver container.
- Specifically, check for the presence of the `--tls-cipher-suites` flag in the kube-apiserver command or args.
- Validate that the value of `--tls-cipher-suites` is set and includes ONLY the strong ciphers recommended by the CIS Benchmark (as listed in the remediation section).
- Pass Criteria: The `--tls-cipher-suites` flag is present and set to the exact list (or a strict subset) of strong ciphers as recommended. No weak or deprecated ciphers are included.
- Fail Criteria: The flag is missing, set to a superset including weak ciphers, or not set at all (defaulting to insecure ciphers).
- Example CLI command:
  ```
  kubectl -n kube-system get pod -l component=kube-apiserver -o jsonpath='{.items[*].spec.containers[*].command}'
  ```
  or, for static Pod manifests (if not managed by a DaemonSet):
  ```
  grep -- '--tls-cipher-suites' /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml
  ```
- The check should be performed on all control plane nodes to ensure consistency.

**SOURCE:** CIS KUBERNETES BENCHMARK V1.8.0 PDF
